scope.question: Can the dualised Standard Model program be presented as a self-contained Part III half-course that builds duality machinery from scratch and produces quantitative IR matching of the SM spectrum?
in_scope: Self-contained ~8 lecture notes on the dualised SM for Part III students
Building duality machinery from scratch without SUSY prerequisites
Quantitative exercises reproducing SM fermion masses, Fermi scale, QCD scale from dual description
Complete LaTeX lecture notes with solved exercises suitable for written examination
Systematic coverage of Seiberg duality, anomaly matching, tumbling, and the dualised SM construction
Full reference corpus from Sannino 2407.17281, Csaki-Terning 1106.3074, Maekawa, and foundational papers
out_of_scope: Full SUSY lecture course — only duality-relevant content if needed
BSM phenomenology beyond the dualised SM program
Cosmological implications of the dualised SM
unresolved_questions: Whether 8 lectures suffice to go from no SUSY background to quantitative spectrum — may need 10-12
Exact chapter decomposition to be determined after literature review

user_asserted_anchors: Physical SM spectrum: fermion masses, v ~ 246 GeV, Lambda_QCD ~ 200 MeV must be reproduced from the dual side
Seiberg original duality results are ground truth for the formal duality structure
context_gaps: Full reference list from 2407.17281 not yet downloaded
Maekawa specific papers not yet identified
crucial_inputs: Exercises must produce actual numerical SM spectrum with physical scales
Notes must be self-contained — no SUSY prerequisite assumed
Solved exercises double as Part III written examination material

formulations: Self-contained lecture notes building from gauge theory basics to dualised SM
allowed_estimator_families: Analytical derivations with quantitative IR matching
stop_and_rethink_conditions: If the dualised SM program does not actually produce quantitative fermion mass predictions
If 8 lectures cannot cover the necessary material even with exercises

observables.name: IR SM spectrum from dual description
observables.definition: Quantitative reproduction of SM fermion masses, Fermi scale v ~ 246 GeV, and Lambda_QCD ~ 200 MeV from the dualised SM framework

observables.name: Anomaly matching verification
observables.definition: t Hooft anomaly matching conditions satisfied between UV and IR descriptions

claims.statement: Self-contained Part III half-course lecture notes that build duality machinery from scratch and produce the physical SM spectrum from the dual description

deliverables.description: Complete LaTeX lecture notes (~8 lectures) on the dualised Standard Model
Solved exercises reproducing the SM spectrum with physical scales, suitable for Part III written examination

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-lecture-notes
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify that exercises produce m_t ~ 173 GeV, v ~ 246 GeV, Lambda_QCD ~ 200 MeV and other SM fermion masses from the dual description
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Physical scales reproduced to correct order of magnitude with correct parametric dependences

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-lecture-notes
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify that a Part III student with standard QFT background but no SUSY can follow the notes from beginning to end
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No unexplained SUSY concepts; all duality machinery built within the notes

references.why_it_matters: Primary reference for the dualised SM construction — defines the duality map, spectrum, and IR matching
Key reference for non-SUSY duality methods and techniques relevant to the dualised SM
Foundational ground truth for the formal duality structure — notes must be consistent with Seiberg results

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-lecture-notes
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Lecture notes that present the formal duality map without quantitative IR spectrum matching
forbidden_proxies.reason: The decisive test is reproducing physical masses and scales, not just the duality structure

weakest_anchors: Whether the dualised SM program actually produces quantitative fermion mass predictions vs. only qualitative/structural matching
unvalidated_assumptions: 8 lectures suffice for self-contained coverage from no SUSY to quantitative spectrum
Non-SUSY duality machinery can be made pedagogically accessible at Part III level
disconfirming_observations: If the dualised SM literature shows the fermion mass spectrum is only qualitatively matched, not quantitatively derived, the exercise framework needs fundamental revision

open_questions: Whether 8 lectures suffice to go from no SUSY background to quantitative spectrum — may need 10-12
Exact chapter decomposition to be determined after literature review
